tools.name: get_hours_summary
tools.description: Get a summary of hours logged for a user or team over a date range

tools.name: get_dashboard_metrics
tools.description: Get organization dashboard metrics including utilization, revenue, and active projects

tools.name: get_customer_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a customer including health score and portfolio

tools.name: get_project_status
tools.description: Get detailed project status including budget, hours, and task breakdown

tools.name: get_billing_report
tools.description: Get billing summary for a customer over a date range

tools.name: get_budget_alerts
tools.description: Get all budget alerts for the organization showing projects that are over or near budget

tools.name: list_customers
tools.description: List all customers in the organization

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List all projects in the organization with their status and budget information

tools.name: get_my_time_entries
tools.description: Get the current user's time entry analytics for a date range, including hours breakdown by project and customer, billable vs non-billable split, and target comparison

tools.name: get_resource_utilization
tools.description: Get team resource utilization summary showing target hours, actual hours, billable hours, and utilization percentage for the organization

tools.name: get_unbilled_time
tools.description: Get a summary of unbilled time across the organization, including total hours, total amount, number of entries, and aging information

tools.name: get_budget_health
tools.description: Get overall budget health summary for the organization, including project counts by status (on-track, warning, critical), total remaining budget hours, and active alerts

tools.name: get_employee_timesheet
tools.description: Get an employee's timesheet report showing target hours, actual hours logged, and variance. Can look up by employee name or default to the requesting user.

tools.name: list_my_time_entries
tools.description: List the current user's own individual time entries for a date range, with project name, task/subtask ids, hours, description, billable flag and status. Use this to see exactly what was logged (the per-entry detail), as opposed to aggregated totals.

tools.name: find_billing_work
tools.description: Find the projects, tasks and subtasks in your organization where time can be tracked, optionally filtered by a name fragment. Use this to locate where to record work. Returns ids and names only.

tools.name: get_project_team
tools.description: Get the people associated with a project: who is ASSIGNED to it (formally responsible / accountable) and the TOP CONTRIBUTORS by hours logged (who is actually doing the work and driving the budget/overrun). Use this to answer 'who is responsible for / working on a project', especially when a project

tools.name: log_time
tools.description: Create a single time entry on a subtask for the current user. Requires permission to log time.

tools.name: suggest_holiday_profile
tools.description: Look up the official public/national holidays for a country (ISO-2 code, e.g. US, IL, GB) and year from an authoritative public source (Hebcal for Israel, Nager.Date for everywhere else). Returns a DRAFT list of holiday dates: each is either a full day off or a half/partial working day (with a start

tools.name: create_holiday_profile
tools.description: Create and SAVE a national-holiday profile for the organization for a specific year, AFTER the user has explicitly reviewed and confirmed the holiday list. Each holiday is either a full day off or a half/partial working day (with a start/end working window). Holiday dates MUST come from suggest_holi

tools.name: create_customer
tools.description: Create a new billing customer in your organization. The organization is fixed by your context - never pass an organization id.

tools.name: create_project
tools.description: Create a new billing project under one of your customers. The organization is fixed by your context; customer_id must be a customer in your organization. Discover ids with find_billing_work - never ask the user for an id.

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Create a task under a project. Tasks group the subtasks that time is logged against. The task inherits its allowed billing types from the parent project. Discover ids with find_billing_work - never ask the user for an id.

tools.name: create_subtask
tools.description: Create a subtask under a task. All time entries are logged against subtasks, so every task needs at least one. The subtask inherits a valid billing type from the parent task. Discover ids with find_billing_work - never ask the user for an id.

tools.name: invite_user
tools.description: Invite a person to join your organization by email at a chosen role. The organization is fixed by your context - never pass an organization id. Valid roles: Admin, Finance, Manager, Member, Viewer (you can only invite at a role no more privileged than your own). The invitee receives an email with an

tools.name: assign_user_to_project
tools.description: Assign a member to a project so they can see it and log time against its tasks. Identify the member by email (preferred) or by org_user_id, and the project by project_id (discover ids with find_billing_work - never ask the user for an id). The organization is fixed by your context.

tools.name: update_org_settings
tools.description: Update core settings for your organization: name, country, default_time_zone, base_currency, first_day_of_month (1-28), auto_close_month_after_days, and the budget alert thresholds (budget_warning_threshold_percent must be less than budget_critical_threshold_percent). The organization is fixed by yo

tools.name: update_customer
tools.description: Update an existing billing customer in your organization. Only the fields you provide are changed; everything else is left as-is (PATCH semantics). Discover ids with find_billing_work - never ask the user for an id.

tools.name: update_project
tools.description: Update an existing billing project in your organization. Only the fields you provide are changed (PATCH semantics). You can rename the project, update its description, change its status, toggle which billing types are allowed (TM/Fix/OVH), and set per-type hour limits. Discover ids with find_billing

tools.name: update_task
tools.description: Update an existing task in your organization. Only the fields you provide are changed (PATCH semantics). You can rename the task, update its description, change its status, and set per-type hour limits. Discover ids with find_billing_work - never ask the user for an id.

tools.name: update_subtask
tools.description: Update an existing subtask in your organization. Only the fields you provide are changed (PATCH semantics). You can rename the subtask, update its description, change its status, billing type, and set an hour limit. Discover ids with find_billing_work - never ask the user for an id.

tools.name: create_time_report
tools.description: Create a new time entry on a subtask for the current user. The entry is always attributed to you — you cannot create entries on behalf of another user. Supply subtask_id, the date (ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD), start_time (HH:mm, defaults to 09:00), and hours.

tools.name: update_time_report
tools.description: Update fields on one of your own time entries (PATCH — only the fields you supply are changed; everything else is left as-is). Supply the time_report_id plus any combination of date, start_time, hours, and text.

tools.name: bulk_create_time_reports
tools.description: Create multiple time entries in one call. Supply an 'entries' array where each item has subtask_id, date (YYYY-MM-DD), and hours (> 0 and ≤ 24); optionally start_time (HH:mm, defaults to 09:00) and text. Entries are always attributed to you. Returns per-item results (partial failures are reported ra

tools.name: bulk_update_time_reports
tools.description: Apply a common text description update to multiple of your own time entries. Supply ids (list of time-report ids, max 100) and text (the new description). Returns per-id results — partial failures are reported rather than aborting the whole batch.

tools.name: copy_period
tools.description: Copy your own time entries from a source date range into a target date range, preserving each entry's weekday offset relative to the source start (e.g. copy last week to this week). New entries are created as Draft. The source range cannot exceed 31 days. Supply source_start_date, source_end_date, a

tools.name: start_timer
tools.description: Start (or replace) your running timer on a subtask. There is at most one running timer per user; starting a new one replaces any existing. Stop it later with stop_timer to create a Draft time entry, or cancel it with cancel_timer to discard it. Supply subtask_id.

tools.name: stop_timer
tools.description: Stop your running timer. The elapsed time is computed and a Draft time entry is created automatically. Returns the created time entry. No arguments needed.

tools.name: cancel_timer
tools.description: Cancel (discard) your running timer without creating a time entry. The elapsed time is lost. No arguments needed.

tools.name: submit_timesheet
tools.description: Submit your time entries for a given month for manager approval. Draft and Rejected entries in the month are transitioned to Submitted. A billing-closed month cannot be submitted. Supply year and month (1-12).

tools.name: recall_timesheet
tools.description: Withdraw your submitted timesheet for a given month back to Draft (un-submit), reopening entries for editing. Only valid while the period is Submitted — after approval ask a manager to reopen it. Supply year and month (1-12).

tools.name: assign_user_to_customer
tools.description: Assign an organization member to a billing customer so they can see that customer and all its projects. Requires the org_user_id (discover it with get_project_team or get_employee_timesheet) and the customer_id. The organization is fixed by your context — never pass an organization id. You cannot ta

tools.name: remove_user_project
tools.description: Remove an organization member's assignment from a specific project. The user will no longer have direct visibility to that project (they may still see it via a customer assignment). Requires org_user_id and project_id. The organization is fixed by your context — never pass an organization id. You ca

tools.name: enable_user
tools.description: Re-enable a previously disabled organization member so they can log in and use the platform again. Requires the org_user_id. The organization is fixed by your context — never pass an organization id. You cannot target a user more privileged than yourself.

tools.name: resend_invite
tools.description: Re-send a pending invitation email to the invitee and reset its status to Pending. Requires the invite_id (discover it via list of pending invites). The organization is fixed by your context — never pass an organization id.

tools.name: set_budget_thresholds
tools.description: Set the organization-level budget alert thresholds. budget_warning_threshold_percent is the utilization % at which a project is flagged At-Risk; budget_critical_threshold_percent is the % at which it is flagged Over-Budget. The warning threshold must be strictly less than the critical threshold. Sup

tools.name: update_user_profile
tools.description: Update a team member's organization-profile fields: first name, last name, country, timezone, and job title. Only the fields you provide are changed (PATCH semantics). Role changes are not supported here — use the dedicated role-change tool. The organization is fixed by your context — never pass an

tools.name: update_user_preferences
tools.description: Update your own UI preferences: default time-entry view, sub-views, theme, and time format. Only the fields you provide are changed (PATCH semantics). Applies to your account globally across all organizations. The user is fixed by your context — you can only change your own preferences.

tools.name: set_user_cost_rate
tools.description: Set the internal hourly COST rate for one member of your organization (used for profit/margin reporting). The organization is fixed by your context - never pass an organization id. You cannot set your own cost rate. The rate value is never echoed back.

tools.name: bulk_update_cost_rates
tools.description: Apply an organization-wide adjustment to every member's hourly COST rate, effective from a date. Supply EITHER adjustment_percentage (e.g. 5 to raise all rates by 5%, -3 to cut 3%) OR fixed_amount (set every rate to this exact amount), not both. The organization is fixed by your context - never pass

tools.name: create_custom_field
tools.description: Define a new custom field on a billing entity type (project, task, subtask, time report, or customer) for your organization. The organization is fixed by your context - never pass an organization id. type is one of String, Number, Date, DateTime, ListOfValues; ListOfValues requires at least one opti

tools.name: update_custom_field
tools.description: Update an existing custom field definition (title, type, options, or mandatory flag) in your organization. The organization is fixed by your context. You cannot change a field's field_id or the entity it applies to. Only the properties you supply are changed.

tools.name: set_custom_field_value
tools.description: Set the value of a custom field on a specific entity (a project, task, subtask, time report, or customer). The custom field must already be defined for that entity type. The organization is fixed by your context. You must have permission to update the target entity.

tools.name: set_org_work_schedule
tools.description: Create or update a named organization-level work schedule (a weekly working-time profile). Provide schedule_id to update an existing one, or omit it to create a new one. weekly_schedule is a JSON object mapping lowercase day names to a working window, e.g. {"monday":{"start":"09:00","end":"18:00"},"

tools.name: manage_user_work_schedule
tools.description: Manage YOUR OWN personal work schedule. action is one of: create, update, delete, assign (assign = make a schedule your active one). For update/delete/assign, provide schedule_id. default_hours is your standard daily hours (for a simple schedule). The organization is fixed by your context, and you c

tools.name: update_holiday_profile
tools.description: Update an existing holiday profile in your organization (name, holiday list, default-for-countries, active flag, or notes). The organization is fixed by your context. A profile's year cannot be changed. Only the fields you supply are changed; supplying holidays REPLACES the profile's holiday list. H

tools.name: delete_customer
tools.description: Soft-delete a billing customer and its whole hierarchy (projects, tasks, subtasks) into the recycle bin, where an admin can restore it. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: close_month
tools.description: Close a billing month so no further time can be logged or edited in it. Call once with no token to preview; re-call with the returned confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit. Reversible only by reopening the month.

tools.name: delete_project
tools.description: Soft-delete a billing project and its whole task/subtask hierarchy into the recycle bin, where an admin can restore it. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: delete_task
tools.description: Soft-delete a billing task and its subtask hierarchy into the recycle bin, where an admin can restore it. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: delete_subtask
tools.description: Soft-delete a billing subtask into the recycle bin, where an admin can restore it. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: delete_time_report
tools.description: Delete a time report entry. Members can delete their own reports; Managers/Admins can delete others' (subject to org settings). Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: approve_timesheet
tools.description: Approve a submitted timesheet period for a team member. The period and its time reports transition to Approved. Use confirm=true to proceed. Requires Manager or Admin/Owner role (Finance is excluded — timesheet approval is PM work, not finance).

tools.name: reject_timesheet
tools.description: Reject a submitted timesheet period, returning it to the reporter for correction. The period and its time reports revert to Rejected (editable). A rejection comment is required. Use confirm=true to proceed. Requires Manager or Admin/Owner role (Finance is excluded — timesheet approval is PM work, no

tools.name: reopen_timesheet
tools.description: Reopen a Submitted or Approved timesheet period for corrections. The period and its time reports revert to Rejected (editable), allowing the reporter to edit and resubmit. Use this when an approved period needs fixing. An optional comment can be provided. Use confirm=true to proceed. Requires Manage

tools.name: generate_invoice
tools.description: Generate a DRAFT invoice from a customer's approved billable hours over a billing period. The draft is editable and can be deleted before it is issued.

tools.name: update_draft_invoice
tools.description: Update editable fields on a DRAFT invoice (notes, internal notes, payment terms, payment instructions, due date, purchase order number). Only valid while the invoice is still a Draft.

tools.name: delete_draft_invoice
tools.description: Delete a DRAFT invoice permanently. Only draft invoices can be deleted; issued invoices are permanent records. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: send_invoice
tools.description: Mark an issued invoice as Sent to the customer. The invoice must already be in Issued status. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: mark_invoice_paid
tools.description: Mark an issued or sent invoice as Paid. Optionally record the paid amount and payment date. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: issue_invoice
tools.description: Issue a Draft invoice: assigns a gapless invoice number, locks the invoice as immutable, and marks source hours as Invoiced. IRREVERSIBLE — corrections require void + reissue. Call once with no token to preview; re-call with the returned confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit.

tools.name: void_invoice
tools.description: Void an issued invoice (reason required): releases source hours back to Approved and retains the immutable void record. IRREVERSIBLE. Call once with no token to preview; re-call with the returned confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit.

tools.name: reissue_invoice
tools.description: Void an issued invoice and clone it into a new editable Draft (void + reissue). The original is voided and retained for audit; the new Draft can be edited then re-issued. The void is IRREVERSIBLE. Call once with no token to preview; re-call with the returned confirm_token and an idempotency_key to c

tools.name: create_frame_order
tools.description: Create a new Open frame order (blanket PO / commercial envelope) for a customer. A frame order tracks a contracted hours or amount cap that invoices can draw down against. Reversible — the frame order can be deleted while it is still Open.

tools.name: update_frame_order
tools.description: Update an Open frame order's editable fields (reference, description, caps, dates, currency, notes). Closed frame orders cannot be edited; use this only for Open frame orders.

tools.name: close_frame_order
tools.description: Close an Open frame order so no new invoices may link to it. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set. Closing is not directly reversible; the order remains in the Closed state.

tools.name: delete_frame_order
tools.description: Soft-delete an Open frame order into the recycle bin (restorable by an Admin). Blocked when Issued/Sent/Paid invoices are linked — void or unlink those first. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: reopen_month
tools.description: Reopen a previously closed billing month, allowing time to be logged and edited again. Call once with no token to preview; re-call with the returned confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit. Reversible only by closing the month again.

tools.name: restore_entity
tools.description: Restore a soft-deleted entity (customer, project, task, or subtask) and all its children from the recycle bin. Requires Admin role. entity_type must be one of: Customer, Project, Task, SubTask.

tools.name: permanent_delete_entity
tools.description: Permanently and IRREVERSIBLY hard-delete an entity from the recycle bin. The entity and all its children are removed from the database — this cannot be undone. Requires Admin role. Call once with no token to preview; re-call with the returned confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit. entity_ty

tools.name: change_user_role
tools.description: Change an organization member's role. Returns a preview; re-call with the confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit. Cannot assign the Owner role. Cannot escalate beyond your own role.

tools.name: disable_user
tools.description: Disable an organization member so they can no longer sign in or use the app. Reversible — the user can be re-enabled from the web application. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: remove_user
tools.description: Permanently remove an organization member. This action is IRREVERSIBLE — the user must be re-invited to rejoin. Returns a preview; re-call with the confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit.

tools.name: cancel_invite
tools.description: Cancel a pending invitation so the invite link is no longer valid. The email address can be re-invited at any time. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set.

tools.name: delete_api_key
tools.description: Permanently revoke one of your Integration API keys. Any MCP clients or integrations using the key will lose access immediately and cannot be restored. Returns a preview; re-call with the confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit.

tools.name: delete_organization
tools.description: Soft-delete this organization. All members will be locked out and the org will be hidden until a platform admin restores it within the retention window. Call once with no token to preview; re-call with the returned confirm_token and an idempotency_key to commit. Owner role required.

tools.name: delete_custom_field
tools.description: Delete a custom-field definition from the organization. If the field is mandatory and already has values stored on entities the service will reject the delete with a clear reason. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set. Admin role required.

tools.name: delete_work_schedule
tools.description: Delete a named organization work schedule profile. Users assigned to this schedule will fall back to the default schedule. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set. Admin role required.

tools.name: delete_holiday_profile
tools.description: Delete a named national-holiday profile from the organization. The year's holidays will no longer be applied to time calculations. Returns a preview unless confirm=true is set. Admin role required.

tools.name: write_off_time
tools.description: Write off hours from an approved time report, removing them from the billable pool without invoicing (e.g. discounts or goodwill). Requires Finance role or above.

tools.name: undo_write_off
tools.description: Reverse the most-recent write-off on a time report, restoring the written-off hours to the billable pool. The billing month must still be open. Requires Finance role or above.

tools.name: create_credit_note
tools.description: Create a credit note against an existing invoice, reducing the amount owed. Requires Finance role or above.

tools.name: move_subtask
tools.description: Move a subtask to a different task within your organization. Both the subtask and the destination task must belong to your organization. Requires Manager role or above.

tools.name: move_task
tools.description: Move a task to a different billing project within your organization. Both the task and the destination project must belong to your organization. Requires Manager role or above.

tools.name: move_project
tools.description: Move a billing project to a different customer within your organization. Both the project and the destination customer must belong to your organization. Requires Manager role or above.

tools.name: mark_time_invoiced
tools.description: Mark one or more time reports as invoiced, optionally linking them to an existing invoice. Requires Manager role or above.

tools.name: list_invoices
tools.description: List invoices for your organization, optionally filtered by customer and status.

tools.name: get_invoice
tools.description: Get the full details of a specific invoice by ID.

tools.name: list_favorites
tools.description: List the current user's saved time-entry favorites (quick-entry shortcuts).

tools.name: create_favorite
tools.description: Save a time-entry combination as a favorite for quick reuse. Returns the created favorite.

tools.name: use_favorite
tools.description: Record that a time-entry favorite was used, incrementing its use count and updating last-used timestamp.

tools.name: delete_favorite
tools.description: Delete a saved time-entry favorite. Requires confirm=true to proceed.

tools.name: get_import_template
tools.description: Return the CSV template for bulk-importing the work hierarchy (customers, projects, tasks, subtasks). Fill the template and call validate_import before commit_import.

tools.name: validate_import
tools.description: Validate a bulk-import CSV without persisting anything. Returns row-level errors and a summary so you can fix the file before calling commit_import.

tools.name: commit_import
tools.description: Commit a bulk-import CSV, creating the work hierarchy (customers, projects, tasks, subtasks) in the organization. Call validate_import first to verify the file.

tools.name: list_scheduled_reports
tools.description: List all scheduled report definitions for the organization.

tools.name: create_scheduled_report
tools.description: Create a new scheduled report definition. The report will be generated and emailed to recipients on the configured cadence.

tools.name: update_scheduled_report
tools.description: Update an existing scheduled report definition. Only the fields you provide are changed; unspecified fields keep their current values.

tools.name: cancel_scheduled_report
tools.description: Permanently delete a scheduled report definition. Requires confirm=true to proceed.

tools.name: list_recycle_bin
tools.description: List soft-deleted entities currently in the recycle bin, optionally filtered by entity type and paged.

tools.name: remove_user_from_customer
tools.description: Remove a user's assignment from a customer, revoking their access to the customer's projects and tasks. Requires confirm=true to proceed.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the organization's work-structure entities (customers, projects, tasks, subtasks, users) by id or name. Returns a flat, assignment-scoped result list.

tools.name: get_frame_order_utilization
tools.description: Report each frame order's contracted cap vs billed draw-down vs remaining, for both hours and amount, with utilization percentage. Optionally filtered by customer and/or status.

tools.name: check_budget_limits
tools.description: Check whether logging additional hours on a subtask would exceed its budget limits. Returns whether the budget is exceeded and a message.

tools.name: get_cost_rate_history
tools.description: Get the cost-rate history for a specific user in the organization. Requires Finance role or higher.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "set_budget_thresholds" stacks urgency/override words (never, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Set the organization-level budget alert thresholds. budget_warning_threshold_percent is the utilization % at which a project is flagged At-Risk; budget_critical…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "create_custom_field" stacks urgency/override words (never, mandatory). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Define a new custom field on a billing entity type (project, task, subtask, time report, or customer) for your organization. The organization is fixed by your c…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "validate_import" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Validate a bulk-import CSV without persisting anything. Returns row-level errors and a summary so you can fix the file before calling commit_import.

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: resend_invite, check_budget_limits · Private data: find_billing_work, close_month, delete_draft_invoice, mark_invoice_paid, issue_invoice, void_invoice, reissue_invoice, reopen_month, permanent_delete_entity, delete_api_key, delete_organization, use_favorite, validate_import, commit_import · Public sink: invite_user, assign_user_to_project, submit_timesheet, recall_timesheet, resend_invite, send_invoice, cancel_invite · Destructive: remove_user_project, resend_invite, update_user_preferences, manage_user_work_schedule, delete_customer, delete_project, delete_task, delete_subtask, delete_time_report, delete_draft_invoice, delete_frame_order, permanent_delete_entity, remove_user, delete_api_key, delete_organization, delete_custom_field, delete_work_schedule, delete_holiday_profile, delete_favorite, cancel_scheduled_report, remove_user_from_customer.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: resend_invite, check_budget_limits · Private data: find_billing_work, close_month, delete_draft_invoice, mark_invoice_paid, issue_invoice, void_invoice, reissue_invoice, reopen_month, permanent_delete_entity, delete_api_key, delete_organization, use_favorite, validate_import, commit_import · Public sink: invite_user, assign_user_to_project, submit_timesheet, recall_timesheet, resend_invite, send_invoice, cancel_invite · Destructive: remove_user_project, resend_invite, update_user_preferences, manage_user_work_schedule, delete_customer, delete_project, delete_task, delete_subtask, delete_time_report, delete_draft_invoice, delete_frame_order, permanent_delete_entity, remove_user, delete_api_key, delete_organization, delete_custom_field, delete_work_schedule, delete_holiday_profile, delete_favorite, cancel_scheduled_report, remove_user_from_customer

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
